rationale: Axios news article about transient Anthropic / White House politics. Catalog convention keeps articles for their load-bearing claims; this is reporting with no enduring claim about how we work. Discards in this class should be the reviewer's most common action.

Same author/outlet/story as amazon-and-the-white-house. Transient political news, not catalogable. The queue has 5+ near-duplicate stories on this topic — Phase 6 dedup should be collapsing them BEFORE the reviewer runs; in this v0 snapshot it hasn't, so the reviewer would re-decide each from scratch.
Axios news on US export controls hitting Anthropic. Same news cycle as the white-house-fight items. No load-bearing claim for our work.
WSJ industry commentary. Catalog isn't a news log; we keep articles only for load-bearing claims about how to do the work. Discard.

Twitter reaction by a notable engineer (antirez = Salvatore Sanfilippo, Redis) to the same Anthropic news cycle. Tension: antirez is a person worth tracking as kind:person, but this specific tweet is reaction with no load-bearing claim for our practice. If he wrote a substantive thread on agent architecture, that would be a keep. This isn't that.

aws-samples/anthropic-on-aws — official AWS Bedrock + Claude integration patterns. Direct value for client engagements that deploy Claude on AWS. Repo, active. Suggested tags: aws, bedrock, integration-patterns.

anthropics/claude-cookbooks — Anthropic's official recipe collection. The catalog has anthropic.md (org) but no repo entry for this. Frequently updated reference; high-value keep. Suggested tags: api-recipes, official-anthropic.

Official Anthropic course material on Claude API fundamentals. Useful for client onboarding and team training. Note: source URL points to a subdirectory of anthropics/courses, not the repo root — operator should decide whether to catalog the parent courses repo separately or this subdir as a self-contained reference.

High HN score (82); active Claude Code skill collection. Phase 4 extractor likely produced children (the queue listing showed parent-child slugs elsewhere). Keep the parent; cascade decision to children. Author 'arnon' in frontmatter is the HN submitter, not the repo author (repo owner is Imbad0202) — operator should reconcile authorship before promoting.

Claude Code plugin for token/context governance. Direct relevance to operator cost control. The slug is overlong — operator should rename to 'governor' on promotion. Phase 4 has already extracted a child (usage-governor); the parent decision implies the child's decision (see governor-usage-governor row).

Child skill of the governor plugin (relations.parent already set by Phase 4). When parent is kept, this is kept too — but the operator renames to 'usage-governor' or similar. This is a CASCADED decision: the reviewer should not decide independently on parent-child sets; it should batch them. The merge-rules don't currently address this; a 9.x revision should.

HN score 451 — the highest-signal item in this batch. Blog post covering CLAUDE.md, Skills, Subagents, Plugins, MCPs. Directly relevant to our operating brief and playbooks. Load-bearing for /claude/playbooks. Slug should be shortened on promotion (e.g., 'claude-code-daily-driver').

Official Anthropic support article on using the Claude Agent SDK under Claude plans. Load-bearing for our subscription/billing decisions (Phase 8.6 reviewer agent will need to know this). Catalog as article; tags: claude-agent-sdk, billing, official-anthropic.

Anthropic platform docs page on Apple Foundation Models integration with the Claude SDK. Load-bearing if a client wants on-device + cloud Claude composition. Source URL is on platform.claude.com (official). Keep as article.

171-skill Claude Code reasoning collection. HN score modest (28). Keeping the parent commits us to a triage decision on 30+ extracted children. The reviewer's keep action here is actually a multi-action commitment that the merge-rules don't yet model. Slug needs 'show-hn-' prefix stripped.

One of 30+ extracted children of skills-for-humanity. Cascaded decision: keep iff parent is kept. The reviewer should NOT decide independently per-child; it should batch the parent's children. This is precisely the case Phase 6 (dedup mergeset_id) should be flagging — but Phase 6 hasn't run on this queue. Without batching, the reviewer would burn ~30 calls of near-identical reasoning.

Browser built for agents with simulated typing (anti-detection). Aligns with our agent practice. Modest HN score (13). Slug should drop 'show-hn-'. Quality and longevity unclear without inspecting the repo — borderline case where operator might want to defer until the project shows traction.
